title: Union of India v. VKC Footsteps India Pvt. Ltd.
legal_principles: Rule 89(5) GST Rules restricts refund of ITC on input services
No refund for accumulated ITC on input services under inverted duty structure
relevant_acts: Central Goods and Services Tax Act 2017
summary: Supreme Court held that Rule 89(5) of CGST Rules, which restricts refund of ITC accumulated on input services under inverted duty structure, is constitutionally valid. Taxpayers in inverted duty structure (where input tax rate is higher than output tax rate) cannot claim refund of ITC attributable to input services, only input goods.
key_holdings: Rule 89(5) limiting ITC refund to input goods (excluding input services) is constitutionally valid
Section 54(3) of CGST Act permits Parliament to restrict refund by rules
Inverted duty structure refund available only for ITC on inputs (goods), not input services
Taxpayers with inverted duty structure must plan their ITC utilisation accordingly
why_important: Major GST ruling affecting manufacturers and traders with inverted duty structures — particularly impacting footwear, textiles, and pharmaceuticals sectors.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Directly relevant to GST refund planning, working capital management, and ITC utilisation strategies.
